question: Do you have a documented access control policy that defines how access to systems and data is granted and revoked?
Do you enforce the principle of least privilege for all user and service accounts?
Do you require multi-factor authentication for access to production systems and sensitive data?
Do you perform periodic access reviews to validate user entitlements remain appropriate?
Do you have an automated process to revoke access upon employee termination or role change?
Do you enforce session timeout and re-authentication policies?
Do you maintain segregation of duties for privileged operations?
Do you log and monitor all privileged access activity?
Do you restrict remote access to authorized personnel with MFA and encrypted connections?
Do you perform static application security testing (SAST) as part of your SDLC?
Do you track and remediate application security findings within defined SLAs?
Do you enforce code review and approval before deployment to production?
Do you perform dependency scanning for known vulnerabilities in third-party libraries?
Do you maintain separate development/staging/production environments with controlled promotion?
Do you maintain a complete inventory of all hardware and software assets?
Do you classify assets by sensitivity level and apply protections accordingly?
Do you have an asset disposal/decommissioning process that ensures data sanitization?
Do you track asset ownership and responsible parties?
Do you have a documented business continuity plan that is reviewed at least annually?
Do you conduct business continuity exercises or tabletop tests?
Do you maintain disaster recovery capabilities including backup and restoration procedures?
Do you define recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO) for critical systems?
Do you have documented procedures for restoring operations after an incident?
Do you maintain a register of applicable legal and regulatory requirements?
Do you perform periodic compliance assessments against applicable frameworks?
Do you track compliance gaps and remediation efforts?
Do you have a process for monitoring changes to regulatory requirements that affect your organization?
Do you have a documented change management policy and process?
Do you require change requests to be reviewed and approved before implementation?
Do you maintain baseline configurations for production systems and track deviations?
Do you enforce infrastructure-as-code practices with version control?
Do you perform rollback testing for critical changes?
Do you have a documented data privacy policy aligned with applicable regulations?
Do you classify and label data based on sensitivity and regulatory requirements?
Do you maintain records of processing activities (ROPA) for personal data?
Do you have processes for responding to data subject access requests?
Do you implement technical controls to prevent unauthorized data access or exfiltration?
Do you deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) agents on all corporate endpoints?
Do you enforce endpoint security baselines (encryption disk patching antimalware)?
Do you manage endpoint configurations through a centralized MDM or configuration management tool?
Do you monitor endpoints for indicators of compromise and respond to alerts?
Do you enforce automatic OS and application patching within defined timeframes?
Do you have a documented information security program with executive-level oversight?
Do you maintain a risk register and perform periodic risk assessments?
Do you assign information security roles and responsibilities across the organization?
Do you track security program metrics and report to leadership?
Do you perform background checks on employees and contractors prior to granting access?
Do you provide security awareness training to all employees at hire and annually thereafter?
Do you have a process for timely access revocation upon employee termination?
Do you include security responsibilities in employment agreements and contracts?
Do you have a documented incident response plan that is tested annually?
Do you have a process for detecting triaging and escalating security incidents?
Do you conduct post-incident reviews and implement lessons learned?
Do you maintain communication plans for notifying affected parties during incidents?
Do you segment your network to isolate sensitive systems and data?
Do you deploy web application firewalls and DDoS protection for internet-facing services?
Do you encrypt data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher?
Do you maintain network access control lists restricting traffic to authorized flows?
Do you monitor network traffic for anomalous or malicious activity?
Do you collect and centralize security logs from all critical systems?
Do you retain security logs for a minimum defined period?
Do you have alerting rules configured for security-relevant events?
Do you perform regular review of security logs and alerts?
Do you encrypt sensitive data at rest using industry-standard algorithms?
Do you manage encryption keys with documented lifecycle procedures?
Do you rotate encryption keys on a defined schedule?
Do you enforce TLS encryption for all data in transit including internal communications?
Do you restrict physical access to data processing facilities to authorized personnel?
Do you monitor physical access with surveillance and access logs?
Do you have environmental controls protecting against fire water and power failure?
Do you assess the security posture of third parties before onboarding?
Do you maintain contracts with third parties that include security and privacy requirements?
Do you perform periodic reassessment of third-party risk?
Do you maintain an inventory of all third parties with access to your data or systems?
Do you perform regular vulnerability scanning of infrastructure and applications?
Do you remediate critical and high vulnerabilities within defined SLAs?
Do you perform periodic penetration testing by qualified assessors?
Do you track vulnerability remediation efforts to closure?
